 {"id":"ft-gofz5.2","title":"Create v0.1.0 tagged release with honest release notes and downloadable binaries","description":"# Goal
Cut the first real version release with honest release notes.

# Design
1. Version bump: Set version = "0.1.0" in relevant Cargo.toml files
2. Write release notes that honestly categorize:
   - Working: observation pipeline, pattern detection, Robot Mode, FTS search, policy engine, fleet memory, tiered scrollback, BOCPD, session persistence, connector SDK
   - Feature-gated: MCP, distributed mode, semantic search, web API, TUI
   - Partial: tx execution (orchestration framework complete, data plane in progress), mission dispatch
   - Known limitations: resource-intensive build, Rust nightly required, WezTerm backend dependency
3. Trigger release.yml workflow
4. Verify downloadable binaries are produced for Linux x86_64 and macOS arm64

# Acceptance Criteria
1. Git tag v0.1.0 exists with annotated release notes.
2. GitHub Release page has downloadable binaries.
3. Release notes are honest — no feature is listed as working unless it passes end-to-end.
4. CHANGELOG.md updated with v0.1.0 section.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:02.879546Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:04:07.025577Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:04:07.022546Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["release","v0-1-0"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-gofz5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:02.879546Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-gofz5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:15.706256Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-gofz5.3","title":"Add CI cargo-install smoke test that runs on every push to main","description":"# Goal
Add a CI job to .github/workflows/ci.yml that runs cargo install from the repo and verifies the binary works, catching install regressions early.

# Design
- New job in ci.yml: install-smoke
- Matrix: ubuntu-latest (x86_64), macos-latest (arm64)
- Steps: checkout → install Rust nightly → cargo install --path crates/frankenterm → ft --version → ft doctor --json
- Timeout: 30 minutes (the build is large)
- Cache: cargo registry + target dir for speed

# Acceptance Criteria
1. CI job passes on both platforms.
2. Job fails if ft binary doesn't produce valid --version output.
3. Job completes in under 20 minutes with caching.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:11.405247Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:01:54.765078Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:01:54.763741Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","regression","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5.3","depends_on_id":"ft-gofz5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:11.405247Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5.3","depends_on_id":"ft-gofz5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:15.917903Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-grz8r","title":"[EPIC] Eliminate verified first-party placeholders and stubs","description":"# Goal
Eliminate every verified first-party placeholder, stub, mock-like runtime branch, and misleading partial implementation surfaced by the 2026-04-02 audit.

Every in-scope item must end in exactly one of two states:
1. Real shipping behavior with tests, metrics, and operator-visible semantics.
2. Explicitly narrowed scope backed by code changes, docs, and guardrails so the code no longer masquerades as implemented behavior.

# Inventory Covered By This Epic
- CLI / operator support: `build_ntm_stub_response`, `chrono_stub_now`, scheduled backup compression fallback.
- Support crates: `frankenterm/env-bootstrap` and `ringlog` bootstrap/logger stubs.
- Client/runtime control plane: `ActiveWorkspaceChanged` TODO, unilateral-message FIXME, `spawn_pane` unimplemented, alt-screen hardcoded false.
- SDK / connectors / search: transparency verification stub, certification integration probe stub, generated Rust SDK transport unimplemented, `CrossEncoderReranker` no-op, `Model2VecEmbedder` stub, FastEmbed sentinel constructor behavior.
- Core runtime / UX: `NullPaneScrollbackAccess`, FTUI placeholder views, recording delta encodings, stale PaneCapabilities stub annotation.
- Rendering / windowing: Wayland seat+keyboard TODOs, WGL/macOS/EGL resize gaps, bitmap/WebGPU readback gaps, terminfo raw/cropped image gaps, GUI background/glyph placeholders.
- Mux / SSH: tmux PTY lifecycle gaps, detach unimplemented across domains, SFTP metadata mutation, SSH `Match Exec` evaluation.

# Scope Boundary
Only first-party source is in scope. Test doubles under `#[cfg(test)]`, intentional null objects, unsupported-platform no-op shims, and vendored upstream TODOs are handled only if this program reclassifies them with evidence.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:26:38.047585Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:31:29.254960Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:31:29.253673Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["placeholder-remediation","stub-audit"]}
-{"id":"ft-jqvg5","title":"Resolve frankenterm-core clippy -D warnings gate","description":"# Workspace-level clippy gate

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 356 verification sweep. The
`frankenterm-core` crate reports **17 clippy errors** when run with
`-D warnings`:

    cargo clippy --no-deps -p frankenterm-core \\
        --features distributed,asupersync-runtime,web \\
        --lib --tests -- -D warnings

All 17 errors are in files **unrelated to ft-xbnl0.2.4**:

- `src/ipc.rs`
- `src/robot_sdk_contracts.rs`
- `src/runtime.rs`
- `src/snapshot_engine.rs`
- `src/ui_query.rs`
- `src/workflows/engine.rs`
- `src/workflows/runner.rs`

These are from recent commits by other agents.

## Why not fix in ft-xbnl0.2.4
AGENTS.md explicitly instructs "NEVER ... disturb in ANY way the work of
other agents." The violations may be:
- awaiting `#[allow(...)]` annotations from their owners
- pending intentional restructures
- already planned for cleanup

Touching them without coordination could override the owners' planned
solutions.

## Fix categories observed (examples)
- `clippy::duration-suboptimal-units` — `Duration::from_millis(2_000)` should be `from_secs(2)`
- `clippy::redundant-closure` — `|| crate::foo()` should just be `crate::foo`
- `clippy::semicolon-if-nothing-returned` — trailing unit-valued expression without semicolon
- Several others (see `cargo clippy` output at time of fix)

## Suggested approach
For each file, coordinate with the owning agent (via Agent Mail or by checking
recent git history for active work) before applying fixes. Prefer:
1. Fixing real issues over `#[allow(...)]` annotations.
2. Single-file commits so each fix is easy to review.
3. Running `ubs` on each file after the clippy fix to avoid introducing
   new bugs in the cleanup.

## Not a blocker for ft-xbnl0.2.4
The ft-xbnl0.2.4-scoped files (`src/distributed.rs`, `src/metrics.rs`,
`src/runtime_compat.rs`, `tests/web.rs`, `tests/ft_xbnl0_2_4_*.rs`) produce
zero clippy warnings.

See: `docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §6.1.1`.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FoggyRobin","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:04:55.194692Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:59:26.995422Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["clippy","code-quality","workspace-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-jqvg5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:05:03.540304Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-kfkyi","title":"DistributedHttpClient must not transparently follow 3xx redirects","description":"# Security: DistributedHttpClient transparent redirect following

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 349 verification work. `DistributedHttpClient`
(built on the default asupersync HTTP client config) **follows 3xx redirects
transparently** by default. A 302 with an arbitrary `Location:` header is
followed without caller visibility.

Current behavior is pinned by `distributed_http_client_3xx_redirect_current_behavior_snapshot`
in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs` (tests module) — the test
asserts `Err` on a 302 whose Location is unresolvable, and explicitly
documents that assertion flipping is the trigger for this bead's work.

## Security concern
`DistributedHttpClient` is used for distributed node-to-node RPC (health
checks, event forwarding). Peer URLs are supposed to point at trusted
nodes. Transparent redirect following means:

- A compromised peer can respond `302 Location: http://attacker.com/` →
  next request body is sent to attacker (including event data, pane
  captures, auth tokens).
- Even without compromise, a misconfigured peer with a redirect loop
  could cause unbounded work.

## Fix
Configure `RedirectPolicy::none()` at the `DistributedHttpClient`
construction site — see `crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs`
around `DistributedHttpClient::new()` and `DistributedHttpClient::plaintext()`.

This forces the caller to explicitly handle (or ignore) the redirect.

## Verification
After the fix:
1. `distributed_http_client_3xx_redirect_current_behavior_snapshot` will
   fail — update it to assert `resp.status == 302` and rename to drop
   the "current-behavior-snapshot" suffix. Rename to
   `distributed_http_client_returns_3xx_redirect_as_ok_response`.
2. Add a second test asserting that a 302 → a different internal host
   is ALSO observable (not followed) — use a second TcpListener in
   the test so the "Location" resolves but the client still doesn't
   follow it.

## Out of scope
- Building a full redirect-following mechanism (if callers want redirects,
  they can opt in explicitly by following the Location themselves after
  inspecting it).
- The asupersync HTTP client's default RedirectPolicy — that's a library
  default and changing it would affect every other caller. The fix is
  to override at the FrankenTerm distributed-client construction site.

## References
- Finding tick: ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 349 (commit 4013f6e9)
- Related doc: docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §5 (out of scope carve-out)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-18T16:59:46.872931Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-18T17:08:05.852811Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T17:08:05.852502Z","close_reason":"Completed by tick 351 (commit 4717c434): DistributedHttpClient::new() now constructs via builder with .no_redirects() explicitly. 3xx responses are Ok(Response{status: 3xx}) observable to caller. Snapshot test renamed + flipped to assert Ok(302). All 40 ft-xbnl0.2.4 tests still pass via scripts/check_ft_xbnl0_2_4.sh.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","distributed","http","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-kfkyi","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-18T16:59:50.751738Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-l9mxa","title":"DistributedHttpClient response-read path does not observe mid-flight cx-cancel","description":"# DistributedHttpClient mid-flight cx-cancel gap

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 380 verification work. When a caller fires
`cx.cancel_with(...)` WHILE a `DistributedHttpClient::get` is in flight (blocked
reading response bytes from a stalled server), the cancel does NOT propagate to
the HTTP client's read loop.

## Reproduction
The ft-xbnl0.2.4 test
`distributed::tests::distributed_http_client_mid_flight_cancel_does_not_hang`
exercises this. Observed behavior:

    elapsed = 10.02s
    result = Err("deadline has elapsed at Time(10000000041ns)")

The elapsed = ~10s matches the test's DEFENSIVE outer timeout ceiling
(`crate::runtime_compat::timeout(10s, client.get(...))`), not the 50 ms
cx-cancel fire. The cancel went through (cx.is_cancel_requested() would return
true) but the HTTP client's await points didn't observe it.

## Relation to other findings
- **Tick 328** documented that `timeout_with_cx` observes cx *budget deadline*
  but not direct cx *cancel*. This is the same shape of gap at a different
  layer — the HTTP client wraps asupersync::http::h1 whose await points
  don't poll cx.
- **ft-kfkyi** (closed tick 351) was a distinct concern (3xx redirect
  exfil). This finding is lower-severity: not a security issue, just an
  operational-ergonomics gap.

## Impact
- Hung distributed RPC calls will eventually time out via internal limits
  (if any configured) but won't observe operator-initiated cancel.
- Agent workflows that fire cx.cancel to abort a slow peer call will wait
  the full internal-timeout window before the call returns.

## Fix approach (candidate paths)
1. Extend `DistributedHttpClient::get/post` to race the inner future against
   a cx-cancel future (tokio::select! or similar race).
2. Patch asupersync::http::h1 to thread cx through its await points and
   honor cancel at read/write boundaries. Bigger scope; upstream change.
3. Wrap the DistributedHttpClient inner calls in `timeout_with_cx` —
   but per tick 328, that only observes budget, not cancel. So this
   doesn't help unless the caller sets a short budget.

Option (1) is the narrowest change; aligns with the cx-first migration
pattern and doesn't require upstream asupersync work.

## Verification
The existing tick-380 snapshot test is the flip trigger. When the fix lands:
1. Observed elapsed drops from ~10s to << 10s (cx-cancel observed mid-read).
2. `result` shape changes from the outer-timeout Err to the HTTP client's
   own cancel-shaped Err.
3. Update the snapshot assertions to require the faster behavior.

## References
- Finding: ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 380 (commit 4d925607)
- Related: `docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §5` out-of-scope carve-out
  for primitive-doc-only coverage (timeout_with_cx / sleep_with_cx — ticks
  328 / 331).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-18T20:12:43.612639Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-18T20:50:48.342908Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T20:50:48.342659Z","close_reason":"Fixed by tick 387 (commit 99640e41): DistributedHttpClient::{get,post} now race inner call against cx-cancel watcher (50ms poll). Mid-flight cancel surfaces as Ok(Err(Cancelled)) in ~70ms. Tick-380 snapshot test tightened to assert the new fast-cancel behavior (elapsed < 1s, exact Cancelled variant).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","cx-cancel","distributed","http"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-l9mxa","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-18T20:12:58.856854Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-n2j5w","title":"Fix MCP module pre-existing test errors under default features and verify 170 test functions pass","description":"# Goal
Fix the known test compilation/runtime errors in mcp.rs that prevent the 170 test functions from running with default features enabled.

# Background
MEMORY.md documents: mcp.rs has pre-existing test errors with default features. The MCP module (3,229 lines, 170 #[test] functions) has comprehensive test coverage. MCP submodules (mcp_bridge, mcp_framework, mcp_helpers, etc.) are declared inside mcp.rs via #[path], NOT in lib.rs — this means feature-gate interactions may affect module resolution.

# Design
## Diagnostic steps
1. Run: cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib mcp 2>&1 | tee /tmp/mcp_errors.log
   (Using build recipe: CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-<agent>-target)
2. Categorize errors:
   - Type mismatches from recent module changes -> update test expectations
   - Missing types/imports due to feature gates -> add #[cfg(feature)] or fix import paths
   - Async runtime errors (#[tokio::test] vs asupersync) -> migrate to sync or runtime_compat
   - Stale test data referencing old API shapes -> update fixtures
3. Fix each error, verifying after each fix that the change compiles
4. Final run: cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib mcp -- all 170 tests green

## Common failure patterns to watch for
- MCP submodules use #[path] declarations — if a submodule file moves or renames, the path attribute breaks
- The 57 tokio-based test failures (ft-22x4r) may overlap — if MCP tests use #[tokio::test], coordinate with ft-682n6
- Feature flag interactions: some test helper types may only exist under specific features (mcp, subprocess-bridge, etc.)
- robot_types.rs changes may have updated MCP response envelope shapes without updating mcp.rs test expectations

## Structured Logging
- Diagnostic log: mcp_test_fix_<timestamp>.log with each error, category, fix applied
- Before/after test counts to verify no tests lost

# Acceptance Criteria
1. cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib mcp passes with 0 failures under default features.
2. All 170 test functions execute (none skipped, none #[ignore]d without documented reason).
3. No semantic changes to test intent — only update expectations to match current API.
4. Diagnostic log documenting each fix for future reference.
5. If any tests require non-default features, they must have explicit #[cfg(feature = "...")] annotations with comments explaining why.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:58:08.516219Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T03:18:06.705038Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T03:18:06.704865Z","close_reason":"Verified: 201 MCP tests pass with zero failures under default features (cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib mcp::tests). The pre-existing test errors documented in memory were likely fixed by other agents. No code changes needed — the 170 test functions mentioned in the bead have grown to 201, all passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fix","mcp","testing"]}
@@ -160,7 +160,7 @@
 {"id":"wa-15fy","title":"CLI commands — wa snapshot save/restore/list/diff/inspect","description":"## Goal
Implement the wa CLI commands for snapshot management: save, restore, list, diff, and inspect. These are the user-facing interface for the session persistence system.

## Background and Motivation
The snapshot system needs a clean CLI interface that both humans and the Robot Mode API can use. Commands should follow wa existing CLI patterns (clap derive, structured output, TOON support) and integrate with the existing wa binary.

## Technical Design

### Command Structure
```
wa snapshot save [--trigger <trigger>] [--label <label>]
wa snapshot restore <snapshot_id> [--layout-only] [--launch-agents] [--dry-run]
wa snapshot list [--limit <n>] [--format json|toon]
wa snapshot diff <id1> <id2>
wa snapshot inspect <snapshot_id> [--pane <pane_id>]
wa snapshot delete <snapshot_id> [--force]
```

### wa snapshot save
- Triggers SnapshotEngine::capture_snapshot()
- Accepts --trigger flag (manual, pre_restart, pre_shutdown)
- Optional --label for human-readable name
- Returns snapshot_id and summary stats
- Robot mode: returns JSON envelope with snapshot metadata

### wa snapshot restore
- Reads snapshot from SQLite
- Phase 1: Layout restoration (always)
- Phase 2: Scrollback injection (unless --layout-only)
- Phase 3: Process re-launch (only with --launch-agents)
- --dry-run: shows plan without executing
- Returns restoration report (panes restored, failures, warnings)
- Robot mode: returns JSON with PaneIdMap and status per pane

### wa snapshot list
- Shows recent snapshots with timestamp, trigger, pane count, size
- Default limit: 10
- Supports TOON format for AI-to-AI usage

### wa snapshot diff
- Compares two snapshots
- Shows: added/removed panes, changed layouts, scrollback size changes
- Useful for understanding what changed between snapshots

### wa snapshot inspect
- Detailed view of a single snapshot
- Shows layout tree, pane details, process info
- --pane filter: show only specific pane data

### Clap Integration
```rust
// In crates/wa/src/main.rs, add to CLI enum:
#[derive(Subcommand)]
enum SnapshotCmd {
    Save {
        #[arg(long)] trigger: Option<SnapshotTrigger>,
        #[arg(long)] label: Option<String>,
    },
    Restore {
        snapshot_id: String,
        #[arg(long)] layout_only: bool,
        #[arg(long)] launch_agents: bool,
        #[arg(long)] dry_run: bool,
    },
    List {
        #[arg(long, default_value = "10")] limit: usize,
    },
    Diff { id1: String, id2: String },
    Inspect {
        snapshot_id: String,
        #[arg(long)] pane: Option<u64>,
    },
    Delete {
        snapshot_id: String,
        #[arg(long)] force: bool,
    },
}
```

## Integration Points
- **SnapshotEngine**: Core snapshot operations
- **Layout restorer, scrollback injector, process launcher**: Restoration phases
- **Robot Mode**: All commands return structured output for AI agents
- **TOON format**: Token-efficient output support
- **wa existing CLI patterns**: Follow same error envelope, --format flag, --stats flag

## Key Files to Create/Modify
- CREATE: crates/wa/src/commands/snapshot.rs
- MODIFY: crates/wa/src/main.rs (add snapshot subcommand)
- MODIFY: crates/wa/src/commands/mod.rs (add module)

## Dependencies
- SnapshotEngine orchestrator (bead 5)
- Layout restoration engine (bead 6)
- Scrollback injection engine (bead 7)
- Process re-launch engine (bead 8)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 6 subcommands work (save, restore, list, diff, inspect, delete)
- Robot mode returns structured JSON envelopes
- TOON format support for all commands
- --dry-run mode for restore shows plan without executing
- Error handling follows wa existing patterns (robot.* error codes)
- Help text is clear and includes examples

## Estimated Effort
3-4 hours implementation, 1 hour testing

## Test Requirements

### Property-Based Testing (proptest)
Add proptest cases in crates/wa/tests/proptest_snapshot_cli.rs:
- **CLI argument parsing roundtrip**: For any valid combination of snapshot subcommand arguments, parsing the args into the SnapshotCmd enum and then serializing back to a command string must produce equivalent args. Strategy: arb_snapshot_cmd() generates random valid SnapshotCmd variants with random snapshot IDs (alphanumeric, 6-20 chars), random limits (1-1000), random trigger types, and random boolean flags.
- **Snapshot ID validation**: For any string input as snapshot_id, the CLI must either accept it (valid ID format) or produce a clear error. No panics on malformed IDs. Strategy: arb_string() generates arbitrary strings including empty, whitespace-only, special characters, and very long strings (up to 10000 chars).
- **Flag exclusivity**: For the restore subcommand, verify that --dry-run combined with --launch-agents produces a plan (not execution). For --layout-only, verify scrollback injection and process launch are skipped. Strategy: arb_restore_flags() generates all 8 combinations of the 3 boolean flags.
- **List limit bounds**: For any limit value in [0, usize::MAX], the list command must either produce valid output or a clear error (never panic, never OOM). Strategy: arb_limit() focuses on edge cases: 0, 1, 10, 100, usize::MAX.

### Criterion Benchmarks
Add benchmarks in crates/wa/benches/snapshot_cli.rs:
- bench_snapshot_list_100: List 100 snapshots and format as table, target <50ms
- bench_snapshot_diff_large: Diff two 200-pane snapshots, target <100ms
- bench_snapshot_inspect_formatting: Format a detailed inspect view of a 50-pane snapshot
- bench_cli_arg_parsing: Parse and validate all 6 subcommand variants, target <1ms

## Cross-References
- **bd-cuz** (MuxSnapshot schema): The CLI inspect and diff commands directly render MuxSnapshot structures. The output format must stay in sync with schema changes. When bd-cuz adds new fields (e.g., agent session metadata, differential snapshot markers), the CLI inspect/diff views must be updated to display them.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:33:10.348567Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-11T04:00:33.685427Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T04:00:33.685427Z","close_reason":"Closed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-15fy","depends_on_id":"wa-1xcz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:35:35.818089Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-15fy","depends_on_id":"wa-29k1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:35:35.491350Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-15fy","depends_on_id":"wa-32z7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:35:36.009363Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-15fy","depends_on_id":"wa-e6pq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:35:35.621383Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-15fy","depends_on_id":"wa-rsaf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:35:07.825290Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-165vw","title":"Integrate tru (TOON format) for token-efficient agent communication","description":"# Integrate tru for Token-Efficient Communication

## Skills: /extreme-software-optimization

## What tru does
TOON (Token-Optimized Object Notation) serialization. 40-60% fewer tokens than JSON. Human-readable, streaming decode, deterministic output.

## Current state
Standalone CLI at /Users/jemanuel/projects/toon_rust.

## Integration plan

### 1. Import tru-core as library
- Use TOON serialization for all robot-mode output
- Dual format: TOON for agent consumption, JSON for human/API consumption

### 2. Robot mode output
All `wa` robot commands emit TOON by default:
```
wa observe --robot --format=toon
wa search --robot --format=toon
```
Token savings compound across hundreds of agent queries per session.

### 3. MCP tool responses
MCP tool responses can optionally use TOON:
- Reduces token usage for large payloads (pane output, search results)
- Agent explicitly requests via format parameter

### 4. Workflow definitions
TOON-encoded workflow specs use fewer tokens:
- Workflow templates stored as TOON
- Agent queries workflow engine using TOON format

### 5. Benchmark (/extreme-software-optimization)
- Measure token savings: JSON vs TOON for typical wa output
- Measure serialization throughput: should be >500MB/s
- Measure deserialization correctness: round-trip identity

## Tests
- Token count comparison: JSON vs TOON for 10 representative payloads
- Serialization round-trip test
- Streaming decode test (large payloads)
- Performance benchmark

## Acceptance criteria
- tru-core as library dependency
- Robot mode supports --format=toon
- 40%+ token savings on representative payloads
- >500MB/s serialization throughput
- Streaming decode works for large payloads

## Test Framework Requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks**: TOON encode/decode throughput benchmarks:
  - Encode throughput: benchmark serializing typical payloads (pane output snapshots, search results, workflow specs) in TOON vs JSON. Measure MB/s for both. Target >500MB/s for TOON encode.
  - Decode throughput: benchmark deserializing the same payloads. Target >500MB/s for TOON decode.
  - Streaming decode: benchmark incremental decoding of a 10MB TOON stream. Measure time-to-first-record and total throughput.
  - Token count comparison: for each benchmark payload, also measure token count (using tiktoken or equivalent) to verify the 40-60% savings claim.
- **Proptest for format roundtrips**: Property-based tests verifying serialization identity:
  - Generate arbitrary Rust structs (using proptest's Arbitrary derive), serialize to TOON, deserialize back, and verify equality
  - Generate arbitrary JSON values, convert to TOON, convert back to JSON, and verify semantic equality
  - Generate TOON strings with random whitespace/formatting variations, verify they parse identically (canonical form)
  - Test streaming decode: split a valid TOON document at random byte boundaries, feed chunks to the streaming decoder, and verify the final result matches single-pass decode

## Cross-References
- **wa-2f2m** (Semantic output compression): TOON provides token-efficient serialization, while semantic output compression reduces the content itself. These are complementary — compress semantically first, then serialize in TOON for maximum token savings. The two beads should share benchmark payloads for consistent measurement.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:12:49.965458Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:47:29.968930Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"wa-16hou","title":"Refactor IPC server (ipc.rs) to asupersync","description":"# Refactor IPC server (ipc.rs) to asupersync

## Background
ipc.rs (~400 LOC) implements Unix socket IPC for CLI-to-daemon communication using JSON-line protocol.

## Current tokio usage
- tokio::net::UnixListener for accepting connections
- tokio::io::BufReader + AsyncBufReadExt for line reading
- tokio::io::AsyncWriteExt for response writing
- mpsc channels (22+ instances) for message passing
- tokio::spawn for per-connection handler tasks

## Migration
1. Replace UnixListener/UnixStream with asupersync equivalents
2. Replace BufReader with asupersync buffered I/O
3. Replace per-connection spawns with scope.spawn
4. Replace mpsc channels with asupersync channels (two-phase send)
5. Thread Cx through accept loop and handler functions

## IPC protocol MUST be preserved
JSON-line protocol (newline-delimited JSON) must produce identical messages.

## Unit tests (LabRuntime + real sockets)
1. **Accept connection**: listener accepts, handler spawned in scope
2. **JSON-line round-trip**: send JSON request, receive JSON response
3. **Multiple concurrent connections**: N clients connect simultaneously
4. **Malformed input**: partial JSON, verify error handling
5. **Connection drop**: client disconnects mid-request, verify clean handler exit
6. **Shutdown**: close listener scope, verify all handlers drain
7. **Channel delivery**: verify IPC messages reach internal channels via two-phase send
8. **Backpressure**: slow consumer, verify bounded channel behavior

Each test logs: connection accept/close, JSON parse events, channel sends, handler lifecycle.

## Acceptance criteria
- IPC server works identically with asupersync
- JSON-line protocol preserved
- Per-connection handlers in structured scopes
- Clean shutdown on scope exit
- 8+ unit tests

## macOS platform note
- **kqueue for IPC readiness**: On macOS, the IPC UnixListener uses kqueue (not epoll) for socket readiness notification. Verify that asupersync's reactor correctly handles kqueue-based accept() and read readiness for Unix domain sockets on Darwin. If any platform-specific code exists in the IPC module, ensure `#[cfg(target_os = "macos")]` paths use kqueue and `#[cfg(target_os = "linux")]` paths use epoll.

## Benchmark requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks for IPC throughput**: Add `benches/ipc_throughput.rs` measuring:
  - Single-client JSON-line request/response round-trip latency
  - Multi-client throughput: requests/sec with N=1,5,10,20 concurrent clients
  - JSON serialization/deserialization overhead per message
  - Connection establishment latency (accept + handler spawn)
  - Comparison with tokio-based IPC server for equivalent workloads

## LabRuntime DPOR
- **Concurrent IPC testing with DPOR**: Use LabRuntime schedule exploration (DPOR) to test concurrent IPC scenarios:
  - Multiple clients connecting and sending requests simultaneously — verify no request/response cross-talk
  - Interleaved accept + handler spawn + response write — verify correct client-to-handler mapping
  - Client disconnect during response write — verify clean handler teardown
  - Concurrent shutdown while handlers are processing — verify all handlers drain before scope exits","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:50:38.399683Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-16T02:03:26.119558Z","closed_at":"2026-04-16T02:03:26.119402Z","close_reason":"IPC asupersync migration complete. Production code routes through runtime_compat (no direct tokio); this bead adds 8 LabRuntime tests in ipc::tests::labruntime_ipc covering mpsc_recv_state_with_cx 3 outcomes (Value/Disconnected/Cancelled), shutdown_signal_pending_with_cx 2 outcomes (true/false), and 3 pure-data serde/auth contract tests. 93/93 IPC tests pass; benches/ipc_throughput.rs pre-exists. Commit 0712534a.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-16hou","depends_on_id":"wa-1bznu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:52:00.632067Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-16hou","depends_on_id":"wa-2abzy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:52:00.522499Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-16hou","depends_on_id":"wa-q8vj3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:52:00.414109Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2707,"issue_id":"wa-16hou","author":"ProudSalmon","text":"Taking a bounded support slice in crates/frankenterm-core/src/ipc.rs only. Focus: stop erasing asupersync mpsc recv cancellation/closure semantics in the IPC shutdown wait path, add focused tests, and keep the scope away from broader Unix socket / per-connection structured-concurrency rewrites.","created_at":"2026-04-01T21:15:18Z"},{"id":2708,"issue_id":"wa-16hou","author":"ProudSalmon","text":"Progress update: tightened crates/frankenterm-core/src/ipc.rs so asupersync mpsc cancellation is no longer collapsed into shutdown/closure during the shutdown poll path. Added focused tests for closed-channel shutdown detection and cancelled-receive non-shutdown behavior. Remote validation is running via rch; the first run caught and I fixed a match-arm binding error in shutdown_signal_pending_with_cx rather than leaving the behavior ambiguous.","created_at":"2026-04-01T21:22:49Z"},{"id":2710,"issue_id":"wa-16hou","author":"ProudSalmon","text":"Completed bounded support slice in crates/frankenterm-core/src/ipc.rs. Result: preserved asupersync mpsc cancellation/closure semantics in the IPC shutdown poll path, added focused regression tests, and validated remotely with rch: cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib --features asupersync-runtime shutdown_signal_pending -- --nocapture passed (2 tests). Remote clippy for frankenterm-core was attempted via rch but failed in unrelated workspace crate frankenterm/dynamic/derive/src/attr.rs on clippy::collapsible_match before surfacing any ipc.rs issue. Earlier cargo fmt --all --check also showed unrelated pre-existing workspace formatting drift outside this slice.","created_at":"2026-04-01T21:38:27Z"}]}
-{"id":"wa-181uk","title":"Replace vendored FastAPI/FastMCP frameworks for asupersync compat","description":"# Replace vendored FastAPI/FastMCP frameworks

## Background
FrankenTerm vendors two async frameworks that are built on tokio:
1. FastAPI — HTTP server framework
2. FastMCP — MCP (Model Context Protocol) server framework

These have deep tokio dependencies and cannot trivially use asupersync.

## Options
1. **Port to asupersync** (high effort, full control)
   - Rewrite framework internals to use asupersync net/spawn/channels
   - Benefit: native integration, no compat overhead

2. **Replace with asupersync-native equivalents** (medium effort)
   - Use asupersync::http server directly for web endpoints
   - Write a minimal MCP protocol handler on asupersync
   - Benefit: clean break, no legacy framework code

3. **Compatibility shim** (lowest effort, worst long-term)
   - Run a mini tokio runtime just for these frameworks
   - Bridge async contexts between tokio and asupersync
   - NOT RECOMMENDED: defeats the purpose of migration

## Recommendation
Option 2 — replace with asupersync-native code. The web server is simple REST endpoints, and MCP is a JSON-RPC protocol. Neither needs a heavyweight framework.

## Acceptance criteria
- HTTP server endpoints work without FastAPI
- MCP tool serving works without FastMCP
- No tokio transitive dependency through these frameworks
- Response format and behavior identical

## Benchmark requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks for replacement framework throughput**: Add `benches/framework_throughput.rs` measuring:
  - HTTP server: requests/sec for simple GET /health endpoint (baseline with FastAPI vs asupersync-native)
  - HTTP server: requests/sec for JSON POST endpoint with body parsing
  - MCP server: tool invocations/sec for a simple tool (baseline with FastMCP vs asupersync-native)
  - MCP server: concurrent tool invocation throughput with N=2,4,8 simultaneous clients
  - Latency percentiles (p50, p95, p99) for both HTTP and MCP operations
  - The replacement MUST match or exceed FastAPI/FastMCP throughput to justify the migration effort.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:50:54.021918Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:51:20.160433Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-181uk","depends_on_id":"wa-1bznu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T05:20:10.447496Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-181uk","depends_on_id":"wa-1u55z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T05:20:03.631211Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-181uk","depends_on_id":"wa-2abzy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T05:20:07.279929Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

## Options

 {"id":"wa-18u","title":"E2E test scenarios: ingest, patterns, workflows, policy, recovery","description":"# E2E Test Scenarios: Core user journeys

## Purpose
Implement E2E tests for all major user journeys.

## Scenarios

### 1. Basic Ingest Scenario
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_basic_ingest() -> Result<()> {
    let mut harness = E2ETestHarness::new("basic_ingest").await?;

    // Start wa
    harness.start_wa(&["watch"]).await?;

    // Create test pane and inject output
    let pane_id = harness.create_test_pane("test-pane").await?;
    harness.inject_output(pane_id, "Hello, world!").await?;

    // Wait for ingest
    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(2)).await;

    // Verify captured
    harness.assert_db_contains("Hello, world!").await?;
    harness.assert_fts_indexed("Hello").await?;

    log_info!(&harness.log_file, "✓ Basic ingest test passed");
    Ok(())
}
```

### 2. Pattern Detection Scenario
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_pattern_detection() -> Result<()> {
    let mut harness = E2ETestHarness::new("pattern_detection").await?;
    harness.start_wa(&["watch"]).await?;

    let pane_id = harness.create_test_pane("codex @ /project").await?;

    // Inject usage limit pattern
    harness.inject_output(pane_id, "\n\nYou've reached your usage limit.\n").await?;
    harness.inject_output(pane_id, "Daily limit resets in 4 hours.\n").await?;

    // Verify event detected
    let event = assert_event_detected(&harness, "codex.usage_limit_reached", Duration::from_secs(5)).await?;

    assert_eq!(event.pane_id, pane_id);
    assert!(event.metadata.get("reset_time").is_some());

    log_info!(&harness.log_file, "✓ Pattern detection test passed");
    Ok(())
}
```

### 3. Workflow Execution Scenario
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_workflow_execution() -> Result<()> {
    let mut harness = E2ETestHarness::new("workflow_execution").await?;
    harness.start_wa(&["watch", "--auto-handle"]).await?;

    let pane_id = harness.create_test_pane("codex @ /project").await?;

    // Trigger workflow via event
    harness.inject_output(pane_id, "\n\nYou've reached your usage limit.\n").await?;

    // Verify workflow started
    let workflow = harness.wait_for_workflow("handle_usage_limits", Duration::from_secs(5)).await?;
    log_info!(&harness.log_file, "Workflow started: {}", workflow.id);

    // Verify steps execute (with mocked responses)
    for step in 1..=7 {
        harness.wait_for_workflow_step(&workflow.id, step, Duration::from_secs(10)).await?;
        log_info!(&harness.log_file, "Step {}/7 completed", step);
    }

    // Verify workflow completed
    let result = assert_workflow_completed(&harness, &workflow.id, Duration::from_secs(30)).await?;
    assert!(result.success);

    log_info!(&harness.log_file, "✓ Workflow execution test passed");
    Ok(())
}
```

### 4. Policy Enforcement Scenario
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_policy_enforcement() -> Result<()> {
    let mut harness = E2ETestHarness::new("policy_enforcement").await?;
    harness.start_wa(&["watch"]).await?;

    let pane_id = harness.create_test_pane("vim").await?;

    // Simulate alt-screen active
    harness.set_pane_state(pane_id, PaneState::AltScreenActive).await?;

    // Attempt send (should be denied)
    let result = harness.execute_send(pane_id, "text").await;
    assert!(result.is_err());

    let denial = assert_policy_denied(&harness, "send").await?;
    assert_eq!(denial.reason, "AltScreen active");

    // Verify audit trail
    let audit = harness.get_audit_entry(denial.audit_id).await?;
    assert_eq!(audit.decision, "deny");

    log_info!(&harness.log_file, "✓ Policy enforcement test passed");
    Ok(())
}
```

### 5. Error Recovery Scenario
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_error_recovery() -> Result<()> {
    let mut harness = E2ETestHarness::new("error_recovery").await?;
    harness.start_wa(&["watch"]).await?;

    // Simulate WezTerm disconnect
    log_info!(&harness.log_file, "Simulating WezTerm disconnect...");
    harness.simulate_wezterm_disconnect().await?;

    // Verify circuit breaker opens
    let health = harness.get_health().await?;
    assert_eq!(health.checks["wezterm_connection"].status, "unhealthy");

    // Verify graceful degradation (no crash)
    assert!(harness.wa_is_running());

    // Restore connection
    harness.simulate_wezterm_reconnect().await?;
    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(5)).await;

    // Verify recovery
    let health = harness.get_health().await?;
    assert_eq!(health.checks["wezterm_connection"].status, "healthy");

    log_info!(&harness.log_file, "✓ Error recovery test passed");
    Ok(())
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 5 core scenarios implemented
- [ ] Each scenario has detailed logging
- [ ] Scenarios use realistic fixtures
- [ ] Scenarios run in CI

## Testing
- Implement scenarios in the standard E2E harness (`wa-w8s`) with per-scenario log files.
- Ensure each scenario writes a summary block (PASS/FAIL, elapsed_ms, key assertions) to the main log.
- CI: scenarios run via the standard runner and emit artifacts to the CI log directory.

 {"id":"wa-1b2n","title":"FTUI-09.5 Post-cutover stabilization and bug-bash program","description":"## Background\nImmediate post-cutover period needs structured triage and fast feedback loops.\n\n## Deliverables\n- stabilization window process\n- bug-bash protocol and ownership routing\n- follow-up bead generation workflow from observed defects\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- high-priority regressions are triaged rapidly\n- stabilization outcomes are documented and closed.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:11:05.114537267Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:39:46.475999251Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:39:46.475865843Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1b2n","depends_on_id":"wa-1i50","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:24:20.603768894Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-1b2n","depends_on_id":"wa-3ue0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:11:05.145341734Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
